variant.handshake_hint: Use tls-token for maximum active-probe resistance (M8).

generation_result.reasoning: 1) Transport uses TLS with Chrome-120 fingerprint to match browser traffic (M2/M3 pass). SNI is set to www.google.com for Suricata compatibility (M2). 2) Handshake uses TLS-token with session_ticket field for PSK auth, leaving PSK empty for evaluator injection (R6). This provides active probe resistance (M8). 3) Padding uses mimicry/chrome-browsing profile to blend with real Chrome traffic patterns (M4/M6 pass). 4) Timing is disabled to avoid artificial patterns (M7). 5) Fallback points to Google to handle probes (M8).

m2_suricata.explanation: Browser fingerprint + valid SNI won't trigger ET rules

m4_ml.explanation: Mimicry padding matches Chrome flow statistics

m6_kl_len.explanation: Mimicry profile closely matches HTTPS length distribution

m8_probe.explanation: Fallback returns legitimate Google HTTPS responses

known_weaknesses: TLS-token handshake may be detectable if session_ticket field is monitored for abnormal lengths
Fixed ALPN list (h2, http/1.1) could be fingerprinted if ALPN randomization is expected
